task: H3 (cross-substrate Phi convergence) + H7c (metric-tractable upper bound) Bayesian zombie posterior numerization
label: anima_8substrate_phi_v3
raw10_caveats: C1: NEG dominance != consciousness absence (metric design artifact possible; HID_TRUNC well-cond regime dependence #161).
C2: N=8 substrate is statistically small; Wilson 95% CI necessarily wide (>= 0.30 width expected).
C3: zombie hypothesis is unfalsifiable in principle (Chalmers 1996); behavioral/measurement equivalence holds by definition.
C4: H7c ceiling (max |phi*| = 16.6959 mistral GPT-NeoX-equivalent baseline) depends on single backbone × single design (HID_TRUNC=8); generalization not established.
C5: convergence definition covers sign+magnitude only; phenomenal axis (qualia, 1st-person report consistency) is untouched by this metric.
C6: Bayesian framework itself uses ad-hoc likelihood assignment (LR_sign × LR_satur); robust posterior would require formal generative model of Phi under each hypothesis.
